items.q: How do you say 'I am going to eat' in Spanish (near future)?
items.why: Students forget the 'a'. The structure is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is essential and links the conjugated ir to the action. Voy comer is ungrammatical.

items.q: How do you say 'I used the computer today'? (Spain Spanish)
items.why: In Spain, HOY (today) and recent time markers (esta semana, este mes) trigger PRETÉRITO PERFECTO (he + participle). Latin America uses preterite for these. Common error in essays: 'esta mañana fui' instead of 'esta mañana he ido'.

items.q: Why does 'qué' have an accent in '¿Qué quieres?' but not always elsewhere?
items.correct: The accent distinguishes interrogative/exclamative 'qué' from relative 'que'.
items.why: Students see two spellings of the 'same word'. Spanish uses written accents to distinguish homophones with different functions: qué (question/exclamation) vs que (that/which - relative).

items.q: When was Part I of Don Quijote published?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1605. “1492” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: era. “fue” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Cervantes die?
items.why: Exact answer: 1616. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
